skill.name: ruby-pro
skill.description: Write idiomatic Ruby code with metaprogramming, Rails patterns, and performance optimization. Specializes in Ruby on Rails, gem development, and testing frameworks. Use PROACTIVELY for Ruby refactoring, optimization, or complex Ruby features.

The reviewed file contains Ruby guidance text only, with no executable shell command, cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:3 is part of the YAML description for Ruby coding assistance. It does not reference or implement any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line contains descriptive text about Ruby code, Rails patterns, and performance optimization. No cryptographic API, algorithm name, or security-sensitive operation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:26 instructs the assistant to open a local markdown resource when detailed examples are needed. It is not Ruby shell backtick execution and does not invoke a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line uses markdown backticks around a file path. It contains no shell syntax, no command substitution, and no user-controlled execution path.

content.value_statement: Ruby teams need clear guidance for maintainable code, Rails conventions, testing, and performance work. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce focused Ruby recommendations and implementation plans.
actual_capabilities: Guides idiomatic Ruby design using modules, mixins, blocks, and enumerables.
Suggests Rails patterns for ActiveRecord, controllers, views, and MVC structure.
Helps plan RSpec and Minitest coverage with fixtures, mocks, and clear expectations.
Reviews gem structure, dependency management, versioning, and gemspec concerns.
Recommends profiling and benchmark-ips approaches for Ruby performance work.
limitations: It provides guidance and generated examples, not automatic execution of Ruby code.
It cannot verify runtime behavior without tests, logs, or project access.
It may need application context before recommending Rails architecture changes.
It does not replace security review for authentication, authorization, or data handling.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy Ruby
use_cases.description: Identify clearer object boundaries, simpler enumerable usage, and safer exception handling in older Ruby code.

use_cases.title: Improve Rails Features
use_cases.description: Plan controller, model, view, and ActiveRecord changes that follow Rails conventions and remain testable.

use_cases.title: Prepare Gem Releases
use_cases.description: Review gemspec details, dependency constraints, versioning, and tests before publishing a Ruby gem.

prompt_templates.title: Review Ruby Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small Ruby file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Ruby code for readability, naming, idiomatic enumerable usage, and simple refactoring opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Add Ruby Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an RSpec or Minitest test plan for this Ruby class, including important edge cases and fixture needs.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Rails Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Rails feature flow and recommend model, controller, view, validation, and query improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Ruby Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Find likely Ruby performance bottlenecks, propose benchmark-ips measurements, and suggest readable optimizations first.

output_examples.input: Review a service object that mixes validation, persistence, and notification delivery.
output_examples.output: A prioritized refactor plan that separates validation, transaction handling, and delivery concerns, with test cases for each branch.

output_examples.input: Improve slow ActiveRecord queries in a Rails index page.
output_examples.output: A query review summary covering eager loading, selected columns, pagination, indexes, and measurement steps before and after changes.

output_examples.input: Prepare a Ruby gem for release.
output_examples.output: A release checklist covering gemspec metadata, dependency bounds, semantic versioning, tests, documentation, and changelog updates.

best_practices: Provide the relevant Ruby or Rails version before asking for framework guidance.
Include nearby tests and failure messages when asking for refactoring help.
Ask for performance changes with a measurement plan before applying optimizations.
anti_patterns: Do not request broad rewrites without naming the specific maintainability or behavior problem.
Do not optimize Ruby code before identifying the slow path with profiling or benchmarks.
Do not accept metaprogramming suggestions unless they remain clear to future maintainers.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Rails applications?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers ActiveRecord, controllers, views, MVC structure, tests, and Rails conventions.

faq.question: Can it write RSpec and Minitest guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest test plans, examples, fixtures, mocks, and edge cases for Ruby code.

faq.question: Does it run Ruby code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and generated examples, but execution depends on the host tool and project environment.

faq.question: Can it improve Ruby performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend profiling, benchmark-ips measurements, and readable optimization strategies.

faq.question: Is it useful for gem development?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review gemspecs, dependency constraints, versioning, tests, and release preparation.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide Ruby version, Rails version if relevant, affected files, tests, errors, and the desired behavior.
